title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Rafael Levi presents a case that agents often falsely claim to have searched the web when they hit blocks, CAPTCHAs, or use stale training data. He demonstrates how Bright Data's Web MCP enables real web access on sites like LinkedIn and Instagram, reducing hallucinations and improving citation reliability compared to agents without genuine external access.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data argues that agents frequently claim to have searched the web when they have actually hit blocks, encountered CAPTCHAs, or fallen back on stale training data. The talk demonstrates Bright Data's Web MCP providing genuine web access on sites including LinkedIn and Instagram, with the claim that real external retrieval reduces hallucinations and improves citation reliability relative to agents lacking it. The core point: an agent's report of having browsed the web is not evidence that it did, and the gap matters for accuracy.
